title: podman pull from docker-daemon does not preserve xattrs
body:
/kind bug
**Description**

With docker buildkit, containers can be built with extended attributes on files and they get preserved within the docker image storage. When importing them to podman from docker daemon, this information is lost. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a minimal Dockerfile
```
FROM alpine:1.13
RUN apk --no-cache add attr
RUN setfattr -n user.pax.flags -v "em" /bin/busybox
```


2. Run  `DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build --no-cache . -t test:latest`

3. Validate 
```
$ docker run --rm test:latest getfattr -n user.pax.flags /bin/busybox    
getfattr: Removing leading '/' from absolute path names
# file: bin/busybox
user.pax.flags="em"
```

4. Import: `podman pull docker-daemon:test:latest`

5. Check with podman
```
podman --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs run --rm test:latest getfattr -n user.pax.flags /bin/busybox                                                                                         :(
/bin/busybox: user.pax.flags: No such attribute
```

**Describe the results you received:**
Extended Attributes are not preserved when importing/pulling

**Describe the results you expected:**
Extended Attributes should be preserved

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.5
Built:              Tue Jan 14 16:02:56 2020
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.13.5
  podman version: 1.6.4
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.12.0-dev
  CgroupVersion: v1
  Conmon:
    package: Unknown
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.2, commit: 65fe0226d85b69fc9e527e376795c9791199153d'
  Distribution:
    distribution: gentoo
    version: unknown
  MemFree: 2325213184
  MemTotal: 16642859008
  OCIRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: Unknown
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc9
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  eventlogger: file
  hostname: xxxxx
  kernel: 5.4.11-gentoo
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 19h 7m 35.83s (Approximately 0.79 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search: null
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 1
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: {}
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 3
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
eix libpod
[I] app-emulation/libpod
     Available versions:  (~)1.6.3-r2^t (~)1.6.4^t {apparmor btrfs ostree +rootless selinux}
     Installed versions:  1.6.4^t(04:03:16 PM 01/14/2020)(rootless -apparmor -btrfs -ostree -selinux)
     Homepage:            https://github.com/containers/libpod/
     Description:         Library and podman tool for running OCI-based containers in Pods
```

**Additional info**
Docker Version is 19.03.5

comments.body:
Thanks for opening the issue, @mrueg!

Podman is getting the image from Docker via the API. It looks like a Docker issue -> https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/40375.

Please re-open if the issue persists once Docker is ready.
